claim: CONFIRMED: YOLO demand router schema readiness ALREADY blocks PRD readiness when any evidence agent reports missing evidence, clarify, block, or failed status. The explorer findings are accurate and independently verified.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() explicitly checks if missing.length > 0 and pushes EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blocker with role and unresolved items
evidence.why: Primary code path converting agent missing arrays into PRD readiness blockers

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() returns prd_ready: blockers.length === 0, meaning any blocker prevents readiness
evidence.why: Final readiness calculation confirms blocker enforcement

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch shows three EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blockers for explorer, cross-checker, and verifier despite all agents returning recommendation=proceed
evidence.why: Runtime artifact proves blocking behavior works in production

evidence.summary: readiness.prd_ready is false, confirming blockers prevented PRD readiness
evidence.why: Actual dispatch result validates the blocking logic

evidence.summary: Explorer agent result shows missing array with 3 actionable items while recommendation=proceed, proving missing data does not force agent-level block
evidence.why: Evidence structure confirms agents can recommend proceed with missing data

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() calls inspectDemandPrdReadiness() with agent_results and merges boundary blockers into final readiness
evidence.why: Dispatch runtime integration confirms evidence agent results feed into readiness

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing() filters out status notes using isNonMissingStatusItem() to prevent false blockers from completion phrases
evidence.why: Evidence normalization prevents status text from becoming blockers

assumptions: The demand requester may be working from outdated understanding of current implementation
The demand may have been filed before the blocking behavior was fully implemented
risks: Proceeding with this demand could duplicate existing behavior or introduce conflicts
Time spent implementing this would be wasted since the feature already works
The current test coverage may not handle all edge cases but the core blocking behavior is confirmed working
missing: Documentation or commit history showing when this blocking behavior was implemented
Evidence of any bug report or issue that would explain why this demand was filed
Test scenarios not already covered by demand-router.test.ts:246-286

result.notes: CROSS-CHECK CONFIRMS EXPLORER FINDINGS. All evidence independently verified: (1) router.ts:496-501 converts missing arrays to blockers, (2) router.ts:750 returns prd_ready: blockers.length === 0, (3) dispatch artifact shows real blocked readiness with three EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING blockers, (4) agent result shows missing data can coexist with recommendation=proceed. The demand requests behavior that already exists. CLARIFICATION NEEDED: Is this a documentation update, test coverage expansion, or is the requester unaware the feature is already working?
